name: ERPSMART Builder Module Removal Safety Contract
purpose: Classify future module removal operations and keep destructive operations forbidden in the current MVP.
current_mvp_rule: Current MVP must not execute uninstall or destructive delete.

delete_draft_definition.data_effect: Removes draft Builder data according to retention policy; no runtime business data should exist.
delete_draft_definition.db_effect: May remove or archive builder definition, version, and preview rows.
delete_draft_definition.implementation_notes: Current implementation deletes only unpublished Builder definition, version, and preview-run records. It does not delete runtime modules, generated files, database tables, migrations, media, business data, or preview artifact directories.

archive_definition.data_effect: Keeps definition, versions, previews, and audit history.
archive_definition.db_effect: Sets archived state or equivalent metadata.

disable_published_module.data_effect: Business data preserved; runtime access hidden or gated.
disable_published_module.db_effect: May write availability/status metadata; must not drop tables.

hide_existing_module.code_effect: Code should not be deleted; UI/routes/menus/permissions should be gated where safe.
hide_existing_module.db_effect: May write configuration or availability metadata.

remove_capability.data_effect: Depends on capability; may involve notes, media, activities, custom fields, search indexes, or automation metadata.
remove_capability.code_effect: May remove or hide generated panels, tabs, actions, contracts, routes, permissions, and frontend components.
remove_capability.db_effect: May require metadata updates, retention rules, or schema changes.

uninstall_generated_module.data_effect: Must choose retain, export, archive, migrate, or destroy data explicitly.
uninstall_generated_module.code_effect: May remove generated files only from a publish manifest.
uninstall_generated_module.db_effect: May alter or drop tables only after backup, approval, and rollback analysis.
uninstall_generated_module.reversible: only with complete backup and rollback manifest

destructive_delete.data_effect: Drops files, tables, media, indexes, and business data.
destructive_delete.code_effect: Removes runtime code or generated assets.
destructive_delete.db_effect: Drops or deletes database objects and records.

agent_notes: Prefer archive or disable before delete.
Do not execute uninstall or destructive delete in the current MVP.
Runtime module removal requires a separate implementation task with approvals, dependency checks, retention rules, backups, rollback, and verifier coverage.
